source: Wikimedia Commons, Openverse-indexed sources, Flickr, and public-domain providers where available
purpose: Investment Analysis syllabus keyword photo pack

items.caption: Supermarket checkout barriers for revenue and sales evidence
items.alt: Checkout barriers at a Sainsbury's supermarket.

items.commonsFile: Modern warehouse with pallet rack storage system.jpg
items.caption: Warehouse pallet racks for inventory and capacity evidence
items.alt: A modern warehouse with high pallet racks for stored inventory.

items.commonsFile: Cash Register-Payment Terminal Store Display - Bergen, Norway 2021-07-30 (01).jpg
items.caption: Payment terminal for operating cash flow evidence
items.alt: A cash register and payment terminal display in a store.
items.credit: Wikimedia Commons / Ryan Hodnett / CC BY-SA 4.0

items.caption: Car assembly line for capacity and capital expenditure
items.alt: Cars moving along an assembly line in a Hyundai factory.

items.caption: Dividend cheque handover for dividend income
items.alt: Officials hold a large dividend cheque during a formal handover.
items.credit: Wikimedia Commons / Press Information Department / Public domain

items.commonsFile: Market centre in Tokyo stock exchange.jpg
items.caption: Stock exchange screen for market value and market capitalisation
items.alt: Trading screens inside the market centre at the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

items.commonsFile: Empty store Loehmanns Plaza Rockville MD 2024-05-25 16-29-42.jpg
items.caption: Empty retail store for demand risk and weak consumer traffic
items.alt: An empty retail store space in a shopping plaza.
items.credit: Wikimedia Commons / G. Edward Johnson / CC BY 4.0

items.caption: Merger valuation chart for valuation evidence
items.alt: A chart showing merger valuation data for Exxon Mobil.
items.deckHint: Use when students need a concrete valuation chart before judging whether a price looks cheap or expensive.
items.creator: Alexeykob
items.attribution: "File:Exxon Mobil merger valuation.png" by Alexeykob is licensed under CC BY-SA 3.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/.

items.caption: Currency exchange-rate board for exchange-rate exposure
items.alt: A currency exchange-rate sign in Amsterdam showing several foreign currency prices.
items.deckHint: Use for currency-risk prompts before students explain exchange-rate exposure.
items.credit: wikimedia / Donald Trung Quoc Don (Chữ Hán: 徵國單) - Wikimedia Commons - © CC BY-SA 4.0 International.(Want to use this image?)Original publication 📤: --Donald Trung 『徵國單』 (No Fake News 💬) (WikiProject Numismatics 💴) (Articles 📚) 14:01, 15 August 2021 (UTC) / BY-SA 4.0

items.caption: Car assembly line for vertical integration and capacity
items.alt: A car factory assembly line inside the Opel factory in Gliwice, Poland.
items.deckHint: Use when students connect owned production capacity to business strategy and operating risk.
items.credit: wikimedia / Marek Ślusarczyk (Tupungato) Photo portfolio / BY 3.0
items.creator: Marek Ślusarczyk (Tupungato) Photo portfolio
items.attribution: "001 Car factory assembly line - Opel factory in Gliwice, Poland" by Marek Ślusarczyk (Tupungato) Photo portfolio is licensed under CC BY 3.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/.

items.caption: Flagship customer-service store for brand equity
items.alt: A flagship customer-service store with service counters and branded signs.
items.deckHint: Use when students connect visible brand presence to pricing power and customer demand.
items.creator: LN9267
items.attribution: "Macau Pass Dynasty District Flagship Store Customer Service Center 13-02-2023(2)" by LN9267 is licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/.

items.caption: Leverage-ratio chart for gearing analysis
items.alt: A chart of Goldman Sachs leverage ratio from 2003 to 2012.
items.deckHint: Use when students need to interpret leverage as a ratio rather than only seeing debt as a headline number.
items.creator: Farcaster
items.attribution: "Goldman Sachs - Leverage Ratio 2003-2012" by Farcaster is licensed under CC BY-SA 3.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/.

items.caption: Subprime crisis diagram for credit-risk chains
items.alt: A diagram showing relationships in the subprime mortgage crisis.
items.deckHint: Use when students need to trace why weak borrowing quality can become credit risk.
items.creator: Farcaster (talk) 05:18, 10 October 2008 (UTC)
items.attribution: "File:Subprime Crisis Diagram - X1.png" by Farcaster (talk) 05:18, 10 October 2008 (UTC) is licensed under CC BY-SA 3.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/.

items.caption: Container stacks for export exposure and global trade risk
items.alt: Multicoloured shipping containers stacked at a port.
items.deckHint: Use when students connect export activity to currency and global-demand risk.
items.creator: Håkan Dahlström
items.attribution: "Multicolored containers" by Håkan Dahlström is licensed under CC BY 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/.

items.caption: Foreign currency notes for translation-effect prompts
items.alt: Samples of Soviet ruble banknotes arranged on a surface.
items.deckHint: Use as a simple visual cue before explaining why foreign-currency results can translate differently.
items.creator: Jorge Lascar
items.attribution: "Samples of Soviet Rubles" by Jorge Lascar is licensed under CC BY 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/.

items.caption: Twitter smartphone network graph for network effects
items.alt: A network graph labelled around Twitter and smartphone connections.
items.deckHint: Use when students need to see that a platform can become more valuable as user connections grow.
items.creator: Marc_Smith
items.attribution: "20110214-NodeXL-Twitter-smartphone" by Marc_Smith is licensed under CC BY 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/.

items.caption: Platform regulation image for regulatory-overhang prompts
items.alt: An image referencing Facebook, Twitter and Google in a political-regulation context.
items.deckHint: Use for prompts about whether platform regulation can weigh on an investment case.
items.creator: sfmission.com
items.attribution: "Facebook, Twitter and Google manipulation by Donald Trump's Election Operatives Raises New Questions" by sfmission.com is licensed under CC BY 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/.

items.caption: Industrial factory emissions for cyclical-sector prompts
items.alt: Factory emissions rising from an industrial site.
items.deckHint: Use as a sector-cycle visual before students discuss demand recovery and operating leverage.
items.creator: Oregon State University
items.attribution: "Factory emissions" by Oregon State University is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/.

items.caption: Federal Reserve Bank of New York for bank capital strength context
items.alt: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York building exterior.
items.deckHint: Use to anchor bank-strength and credit-risk discussion in a real financial institution setting.
items.creator: epicharmus
items.attribution: "Federal Reserve Bank of New York Building panorama" by epicharmus is licensed under CC BY 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/.

items.caption: Digital in-store price display for pricing-power prompts
items.alt: A wireless in-store price display at a clothing retailer.
items.deckHint: Use when students decide whether a company can raise or maintain prices without losing customers.
items.creator: Tomwsulcer
items.attribution: "Wireless in-store price display at a clothing retailer in NJ" by Tomwsulcer is marked with CC0 1.0. To view the terms, visit https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/deed.en/.

items.caption: GSK building for multinational-company exposure
items.alt: The exterior of a GSK corporate building.
items.deckHint: Use when students distinguish local and global risks for a multinational company.
items.credit: flickr / Peter O'Connor aka anemoneprojectors / BY-SA 2.0
items.creator: Peter O'Connor aka anemoneprojectors
items.attribution: "GOC Kew to Osterley 085: GSK" by Peter O'Connor aka anemoneprojectors is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/.

items.caption: Store closure for turnaround and recovery analysis
items.alt: A closed electronics retail store after employees lost their jobs.
items.deckHint: Use for turnaround prompts where students judge whether weak stores can recover.
items.creator: infomatique
items.attribution: "Peats 'World Of Electronics' - 75 Employees Lose Their Jobs" by infomatique is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/.

items.caption: Smartphone user group for active users
items.alt: A group of diverse people using smartphones.
items.deckHint: Use for active-user and platform-business prompts before students judge monetisation potential.
items.creator: Rawpixel Ltd
items.attribution: "Group of diverse people using smartphones" by Rawpixel Ltd is licensed under CC BY 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/.

items.caption: Mobile payment QR scan for ARPU and monetisation
items.alt: A smartphone scanning a QR code for mobile payment.
items.deckHint: Use when students connect active users to payment, revenue per user and monetisation.
items.creator: pr_ip
items.attribution: "Mobile Payment: QR-code scan (300 dpi)" by pr_ip is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/.

items.caption: Debt clock for debt and leverage discussion
items.alt: A public debt clock showing changing debt figures.
items.deckHint: Use for debt and leverage prompts before students judge whether borrowing increases risk.
items.creator: pthread1981
items.attribution: "German Debt Clock" by pthread1981 is licensed under CC BY 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/.

items.caption: Statement of cash flows for financial-statement source work
items.alt: A statement of cash flows page for trust funds.
items.deckHint: Use when students need to see that cash-flow evidence comes from a specific statement.
items.creator: Ladyfwr
items.attribution: "File:11 Statement Cash Flows Trust Funds.jpg" by Ladyfwr is licensed under CC BY-SA 3.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/.

items.caption: Federal Reserve open board meeting for interest-rate context
items.alt: Federal Reserve officials seated at an open board meeting.
items.deckHint: Use when students connect bank interest margins and credit risk to central-bank rate context.
items.creator: Federalreserve
items.attribution: "May 3, 2016 Open Board Meeting: _DSC1408" by Federalreserve is marked with Public Domain Mark 1.0. To view the terms, visit https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/mark/1.0/.

items.caption: Government-policy chart for policy-risk discussion
items.alt: A chart linking government policies and the subprime mortgage crisis.
items.deckHint: Use for policy-risk prompts where students must decide what the chart can and cannot prove.
items.creator: deborahgomez
items.attribution: "Government policies and the subprime mortgage crisis" by deborahgomez is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/.

items.caption: Transmission lines for defensive utility business evidence
items.alt: Electric transmission lines carrying power across a utility grid.
items.deckHint: Use for defensive-business prompts where students compare stable utility demand with cyclical demand.
items.creator: Chris Hunkeler
items.attribution: "Transmission Lines" by Chris Hunkeler is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/.

items.caption: Inspection scene for quality-company prompts
items.alt: A USDA inspection or quality-control scene in a production setting.
items.deckHint: Use when students connect quality control and execution to company quality.
items.creator: USDAgov
items.attribution: "201107120-RD-LSC-0117" by USDAgov is licensed under CC BY 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/.

items.caption: Sale sign for pricing power and discounting
items.alt: A sale sign advertising discounts in a shopping mall.
items.deckHint: Use when students decide whether discounts signal weak pricing power or a normal retail tactic.
items.creator: Artem Beliaikin
items.attribution: "Sale sign in the shopping mall. Discount tag. Bangkok." by Artem Beliaikin is marked with CC0 1.0. To view the terms, visit https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/.

items.caption: National debt clock for leverage risk
items.alt: The National Debt Clock in New York showing large debt figures.
items.deckHint: Use as an alternative debt visual when comparing debt scale and leverage risk.
items.creator: Sugarmonster
items.attribution: "National Debt Clock - New York" by Sugarmonster is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/.

items.caption: Financial paperwork for liabilities and statement discipline
items.alt: A tax or financial form being filled out by hand.
items.deckHint: Use for statement-discipline prompts where students identify what source a figure came from.
items.creator: kenteegardin
items.attribution: "IRS 1040 Tax Form Being Filled Out" by kenteegardin is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0. To view a copy of this license, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/.

sourceAccessNotes: Openverse, Wikimedia Commons, Flickr-origin open media and government/public-domain records were usable from the local CLI.
Pexels web search returned a JavaScript/browser challenge from the local CLI, so no Pexels item was registered without a verified source URL.
Unsplash API search requires a client id and returned 401 from the local CLI, so no Unsplash item was registered without a verified source URL.
